name: renameFile
description: Rename an existing file to a new path within the base directory. Fails if source doesn't exist or target already exists.
intent: rename a file from one path to another
examples: Change the file name of docs/old.md to docs/new.md
whenToUse: The user wants to change a file's path but keep its location and contents intact.
Both source and target sit inside the same base directory (no cross-directory rename).
The new path must not already exist (the command fails on collision instead of overwriting).
whenNotToUse: The user wants to move the file to a different parent directory. Use moveFile instead.
The file should be relocated across devices. Use moveFile instead (it does copy-then-delete).
The user wants to read, discover, or delete the file. Use readFile / glob instead.

oldPath.description: Current relative path of the file to rename

newPath.description: New relative path for the file
